nodes.label: pyDataSourceList entry — AggregateSources
nodes.description: Minimum-viable aggregate-sources source entry. Combines results from multiple independent sub-sources into a single data page. Required field pyAggregatedDataSourceList (each nested entry is itself a DeclarePageSource of any type). Rare — used when one data page must merge multiple feeds.

nodes.label: pyDataSourceList entry — Connector
nodes.description: Minimum-viable connector source entry. Required fields pyConnectorName, pyConnectorClassName, pyConnectorList; typically accompanied by request/response data transforms. The bound connector's py{METHOD}ResponseDataList[*].pyMapToKey must use 'DataSource.<property>' (e.g. 'DataSource.pyResponseData') for the response DT to see the body. pyResDataTransform must be a clipboard-format wrapper DT, not a JSON-format DT directly.

nodes.label: pyDataSourceList entry — Connector with parameter mapping
nodes.description: Connector source entry with pyConnectorParamList mapping data page parameters to connector query-string parameters. pyIsActivityParameter must be "false" for connector params (query string, path, headers); "true" only for pxCallConnector activity-level overrides.

nodes.label: pyDataSourceList entry — DataTransform
nodes.description: Minimum-viable data-transform source entry. Simplest source type; only pyDTName is required.

nodes.label: pyDataSourceList entry — GenAI
nodes.description: Minimum-viable GenAI source entry. Loads data via a Generative AI connector (Rule-Connect-GenerativeAI). Required field pyConnectorName. Server auto-sets pyLoadActivity to pxCallGenAI.

nodes.label: pyDataSourceList entry — KnowledgeBuddy

nodes.label: pyDataSourceList entry — LoadActivity

nodes.label: pyDataSourceList entry — ObjOpen
nodes.description: Minimum-viable ObjOpen (Lookup) source entry. Retrieves a single record by key. Required fields pyLookupClassName and pyClassKeyValueList. Server auto-sets pyLoadActivity to pxCallObjOpen.

nodes.label: pyDataSourceList entry — ReportDefinition
nodes.description: Minimum-viable report-definition source entry. Required fields pyLoadReportDefinition and pyReportDefinitionClass. Server auto-sets pyLoadActivity to pxCallRetrieveReportData.

nodes.label: pyDataSourceList entry — RoboticAutomation
nodes.description: Minimum-viable Robotic Automation data page source entry. Triggers a server-side robotic automation to load data. Required field pyRACaseType. Optional pyRAReqDTName, pyRARespDTName, pyRATimeout. Server auto-sets pyLoadActivity to pxCallRoboticAutomation.

nodes.label: pyDataSourceList entry — RoboticDesktopAutomation
nodes.description: Minimum-viable Robotic Desktop Automation (RDA) data page source entry. Triggers a desktop robotic automation to load data. Required field pyRDAAutomationId. Shares RA fields (pyRAReqDTName, pyRARespDTName, pyRATimeout). Server auto-sets pyLoadActivity to pxCallRoboticDesktopAutomation.

nodes.label: pyParameters entry — DATE
nodes.description: Date-typed data page parameter (no time component). Used for calendar-day filters and date-range endpoints.

nodes.label: pyParameters entry — DATETIME
nodes.description: DateTime-typed data page parameter (date + time). Used for point-in-time snapshots and timestamp filters.

nodes.label: pyParameters entry — DECIMAL
nodes.description: Decimal-typed data page parameter (fixed-precision). Used for monetary amounts, percentages, and other exact numeric values.

nodes.label: pyParameters entry — DOUBLE
nodes.description: Double-precision floating-point data page parameter. Used for scientific or statistical values where decimal precision is not critical.

nodes.label: pyParameters entry — INTEGER
nodes.description: Integer-typed data page parameter. Used for counts, limits, page sizes, numeric IDs stored as whole numbers.

nodes.label: pyParameters entry — PAGE
nodes.description: Page-typed data page parameter (structured embedded page). Requires pyParametersParamIntelliBaseClass to identify the page class.

nodes.label: pyParameters entry — STRING
nodes.description: String-typed data page parameter. The most common type; used for IDs, names, codes, free-text selectors.

nodes.label: pyParameters entry — TIMEOFDAY
nodes.description: Time-of-day-typed data page parameter (no date component). Used for recurring-schedule windows and business-hours filters.

nodes.label: pyParameters entry — TRUEORFALSE
nodes.description: Boolean-typed data page parameter. Values are the literal strings "true" / "false".

nodes.label: Read-Only Connector-Backed Data Page
nodes.description: Complete read-only (non-savable) data page sourced from a REST connector. Uses pyPageType "normal" which derives pyType="normal", pyIsSavable="false", pyUseNewGenPageForEditableDataPage="false". Do NOT use pyPageType "loadonly" — it causes the page to appear editable in the UI.

nodes.label: Savable Data Page (simplesave)

nodes.label: Savable Data Page with Multi-Option Save Plan

nodes.label: Simulated Data Page — Mock / Fake / Test Data Replacement
nodes.description: Simulated (a.k.a. mock, fake, stub, test-data) data page that replaces a real connector source with a DataTransform providing canned data. The original Connector source is preserved verbatim in pyDisabledSource so the simulation can be reversed. Ruleset layering activates the simulation per environment — transparent to callers. Not to be confused with unit-test mocks (Rule-Test-Unit-Case.pySetupPages). See `references/simulation.md` for the full pattern, constraints, and pitfalls.

nodes.description: Case type with starting flows (pyCasetypeStartingFlows, pyFlowsToStart), stage-level and case-wide optional processes, multi-channel stage configuration, and case parameters.

nodes.label: Approval with SLA
nodes.description: Case type with manual stage transitions, case-level and stage-level SLAs, approval step type, optimistic locking, and stage-level attachments with required categories.

nodes.label: Auto Transition
nodes.description: Case type with automatic stage transitions and conditional process execution using when-rules.

nodes.label: Complex with Local Actions
nodes.description: Case type with case-wide and stage-level local actions, child cases with property mapping, conditional processes, multi-process stages, and expression-based skip conditions.

nodes.label: Parent Child Dependencies
nodes.description: Case type with multiple child case types using both auto-start on parent creation and dependency fulfillment, required children blocking resolution, data transforms, and status groups.

nodes.label: Pipeline with Params
nodes.description: Data pipeline case type with parameterized processes, conditional stage skipping via when-rules, and process-level SLAs.

nodes.label: Default Channel
nodes.description: Single visible Default channel — the standard configuration when no multi-channel intake is needed.

nodes.label: Email Channel
nodes.description: Email-intake channel entry. Cases created from inbound email listeners route through processes scoped to pyChannelName='Email'.

nodes.label: Hidden Channel
nodes.description: Channel defined but not currently active. pyShowChannel='false' keeps the channel configuration in place while suppressing its processes at runtime.

nodes.label: Mobile Channel
nodes.description: Mobile-app channel entry for cases initiated from a Pega mobile client. Routes to mobile-specific create flows when paired with channel-scoped processes.

nodes.label: Multi Channel
nodes.description: A pyChannels array containing multiple channel entries inside a single stage. Each channel can host its own set of channel-scoped processes (matched via pyChannelName on Embed-StageProcess).

nodes.label: Web Channel
nodes.description: Web (browser/portal) channel entry. Pair with Embed-StageProcess entries whose pyChannelName='Web' to scope processes to web intake.

nodes.label: Channel Specific
nodes.description: Process bound to a specific channel via pyChannelName. Only runs when the case arrives through that channel.

nodes.label: Conditional Expression
nodes.description: Process gated by an inline expression rather than a when-rule. Uses pySkipOrAllowType='expression'.

nodes.label: Conditional Skip When For Process
nodes.description: Process that is SKIPPED when a when-rule evaluates true (inverse of pyStartWhen). Uses pyWhenToSkip.

nodes.label: Conditional When
nodes.description: Process that runs only when a when-rule evaluates true. Uses pySkipOrAllowType='when' with pyStartWhen referencing the when-rule.

nodes.label: Create Form
nodes.description: Standard create form process used in initialization stages. References the platform CreateForm_Default flow and does not relaunch on reentry.

nodes.label: Optional Adhoc
nodes.description: Process configured for stage-level pyOptionalProcesses array — operators launch it manually from the actions menu rather than it starting automatically.

nodes.label: Parallel Basic
nodes.description: Process that starts immediately when the stage is entered. The first process in a stage is typically PARALLEL.

nodes.label: Sequential Basic
nodes.description: Process that starts only after preceding processes in the stage have completed. Used to enforce order within a multi-process stage.

nodes.label: With Call Params
nodes.description: Process passing input parameter values to its flow at start time via pyCallParams. Same flow can be reused across stages with different values.

nodes.label: With Process SLA
nodes.description: Process configured with a process-level SLA. pySLAType='Always' attaches the SLA rule named in pyStepSLA.

nodes.label: With Rule Parameters
nodes.description: Process declaring its parameter interface via pzRuleParameters (name, type, description, required flag). pyCallParams supplies the values; pzRuleParameters describes the contract.

nodes.label: Alternate Rejected
nodes.description: Second alternate stage (ALT2) for rejection path. Terminal with Resolved-Rejected status; does not cascade child resolution.

nodes.label: Alternate Withdrawn
nodes.description: Alternate stage for withdrawal/cancellation. Uses ALT1 ID, resolution transition, and cascades cancellation to child cases.

nodes.label: Automatic Transition
nodes.description: Mid-lifecycle stage that auto-advances when all processes complete. Sets pyStageEntryStatus to mark the case as in-progress.

nodes.label: Conditional Skip Expression
nodes.description: Stage gated by an inline expression rather than a when-rule. Uses pySkipOrAllowType='expression' with pyExpressionToSkipOrAllow.

nodes.label: Conditional Skip When For Stage
nodes.description: Stage that is conditionally skipped based on a when-rule. Uses pySkipOrAllowType='when' with pySkipStageWhen referencing the rule.

nodes.label: Initialization Stage
nodes.description: First primary stage entered during case creation. Marked with pyIsInitializationStage and contains the create form process.

nodes.label: Manual Transition
nodes.description: Stage that waits for an explicit user/system action to advance. Used for approval gates or external dependencies.

nodes.label: Multi Process Pipeline
nodes.description: Single stage running two processes — one PARALLEL (starts immediately) and one SEQUENTIAL (waits for the parallel process to finish).

nodes.label: Terminal Resolution
nodes.description: Final primary stage that resolves the case. Sets pyStageTransition='resolution', pyIsTerminalStage='true', a resolved status, and runs the cleanup process.

nodes.label: With Local Actions
nodes.description: Stage exposing one or more stage-scoped local actions (visible only while the case is in this stage). Supplements case-wide local actions.

nodes.label: With Required Attachments
nodes.description: Stage that blocks advancement until specific attachment categories have at least one attachment. Combines pyAttachments with pyRequiredCategories.

nodes.label: With Stage SLA
nodes.description: Stage configured with a stage-level SLA via pySLAType='Always'. The actual goal/deadline intervals live in the referenced SLA rule.

nodes.label: With Validation
nodes.description: Stage that runs a validate rule on entry via pyValidate. If validation fails, the transition into the stage is blocked.

nodes.label: Simple Linear
nodes.description: Case type with three primary stages (Create, Fulfillment, Completed) and one alternate stage (Withdrawn).

nodes.label: Stub Casetype
nodes.description: Minimal case type definition — smallest valid create payload with a single stage.

nodes.label: Managing Lifecycle Stages
nodes.description: Procedures for inserting, renaming, changing the flow of, reordering, and removing stages on an existing case type — including renumbering, supporting-rule dependencies, verification, and troubleshooting.

nodes.label: Abstract Class
nodes.description: Abstract class without a trailing dash -- parent for a family of API or utility classes.

nodes.label: Data Class Definition
nodes.description: Data class under the application's data pool.

nodes.label: Embed Class
nodes.description: Concrete embedded class deriving from Embed- -- used for structured page lists and page groups.

nodes.label: Class Group Root (ISCLASSGROUP)
nodes.description: A class that IS the class group root — subclasses use HASCLASSGROUP pointing back to this class.

nodes.label: Stub Class
nodes.description: Minimal class definition — smallest valid create payload.

nodes.label: Work Subclass
nodes.description: Work class with class group assignment — standard Constellation app pattern.

nodes.label: attach-summary-report-to-case
nodes.description: Load when you need Mail that attaches generated output to the case; contains a source-only attachment example.

nodes.label: mail-with-word-template
nodes.description: Load when you need Mail generated from a Word template; contains a Rule-Template-Word reference.

nodes.label: Caption Label

nodes.label: GenAI System Prompt
nodes.description: System prompt field value for a GenAI connector (pyGenerativeAISystemPrompt). Referenced by pyGenAIDef.pySystemPrompt in a Rule-Connect-GenerativeAI connector rule.

nodes.label: GenAI User Prompt
nodes.description: User prompt field value for a GenAI connector (pyGenerativeAIPrompt). Referenced by pyGenAIDef.pyUserPrompt in a Rule-Connect-GenerativeAI connector rule.

nodes.label: Message Label
nodes.description: A message label field value used for API or UI error/status messages. Applied to @baseclass so it is available across all classes.

nodes.label: Notification Message
nodes.description: Notification message field value used with notification shapes in flows. Applied to a work class for case-specific notification content.

nodes.label: Parameterized Message
nodes.description: Field value with placeholder parameters -- pyLocalizedValue uses {1}, {2} placeholders substituted at runtime via pyFieldValueParams. Shows pyValue as parameter key and pyDefault as property reference.

nodes.label: Property Reference Expression
nodes.description: Field value with an inline Pega property reference expression in pyLocalizedValue -- resolved directly from the clipboard at runtime without pyFieldValueParams.

nodes.label: Stub Field Value
nodes.description: Minimal field value -- smallest valid create payload with only the required fields.

nodes.description: Load when wiring connectors between flow shapes — setting condition types, expressions, likelihoods, or naming connector IDs. Also covers server-written boilerplate and constraint points.

nodes.label: enhancing-steps-workflow
nodes.description: Load when modifying what an existing flow shape does — changing its backing FlowAction, Activity, Decision rule, or Correspondence — without adding or removing shapes.

nodes.label: flow-creation-workflow
nodes.description: Load when creating a brand-new flow from scratch. Covers choosing category and structure type, setting rule-level fields, wiring initial shapes, and post-creation verification.

nodes.label: flowaction-wiring-patterns
nodes.description: Load when creating or configuring a FlowAction for a flow's Assignment shape. Covers how FlowActions connect to their screen views, which fields to set, and common pitfalls like pyconfirmchoice casing.

nodes.label: legacy-shape-ids
nodes.description: Load when updating or recreating a pre-2020 imported flow that uses semantic shape IDs like FLOWEND, Fork592, or UTILITY12 instead of modern auto-generated IDs.

nodes.label: routing-assignment

nodes.label: routing-decision

nodes.label: rule-level-fields
nodes.description: Load when copying or recreating a flow and encountering unfamiliar rule-level fields. Documents UI flags, modeler state, and metadata fields that the server writes but agents typically omit on create.

nodes.label: shapes-decision
nodes.description: Load when authoring a Decision shape in a flow (any variant — when-rule, property compare, table/tree-backed) — covers required fields, connector wiring, and type-specific gotchas.

nodes.label: shapes-genai
nodes.description: Load when wiring a GenerativeAI shape in a flow to an AI connector. Covers the two-rule bundle (connector + prompt), shape fields, and placeholder vs configured states.

nodes.label: shapes-parallel
nodes.description: Load when authoring parallel execution in a flow — SplitForEach iteration, SplitJoin parallel branches, Fork shapes, and their pyFromTasks wiring.

nodes.label: shapes-start-end
nodes.description: Load when authoring Start or End shapes in a flow — covers field subsets for each, ScreenFlow Start variant, server-written extras, and Change-to-Stage End wiring.

nodes.label: shapes-subprocess
nodes.description: Load when authoring a SubProcess or Spinoff shape in a flow — covers shape fields, parameter passing, the Spinoff dedicated subclass, and Annotation shapes.

nodes.label: shapes-utility
nodes.description: Load when authoring a Utility shape in a flow — covers smart-shape variants (data transform, notification, change-to-stage), plain-activity wiring, and field differences between variants.

nodes.label: top-level-rollups
nodes.description: Load when a flow needs rule-level summary lists that mirror shape-level data — pyUseCaseLinks, pyCoveredBy, pyTaskInfo, and pxDataReferences rollup mechanics.

nodes.label: Connector Action (Assignment Shape)
nodes.description: Flow action wired to an assignment shape connector in a flow. Uses pyUsedAs LOCALANDCONNECTOR with STANDARD container type. This is the pattern needed when adding a new assignment step to a flow.

nodes.label: Guarded Action with Lifecycle Hooks
nodes.description: Flow action with pre-processing activity, validation, data transform, privilege guard, when guard, and status update. Shows how to override auto-filled defaults like pyUpdateStatus.

nodes.label: Post-Processing Activity

nodes.label: Post-Processing Data Transform
nodes.description: Flow action that uses a post-processing data transform (pyActionTransformRule) to compute or set values after the user submits the form but before the case advances.

nodes.label: Combined Pre and Post Processing
nodes.description: Flow action using both pre-processing (activity to load data) and post-processing (data transform to set values, validation activity to check business rules, local action activity to execute). Demonstrates the full lifecycle hook chain.

nodes.label: Pre-Processing Activity
nodes.description: Flow action that uses a pre-processing activity (pyPreProcessingActivity) to load external data or perform complex initialization before the form renders.

nodes.label: Pre-Processing Data Transform
nodes.description: Flow action that uses a pre-processing data transform (pyPreProcessingTransformRule) to initialize or populate fields before the form is displayed to the user.

nodes.label: Stub Flow Action
nodes.description: Minimal flow action — smallest valid create payload for Cosmos/Constellation apps. Code generation, design template, button labels, and behavioral defaults are all auto-filled by the schema.

nodes.label: Simple Local Action (Traditional HTML)
nodes.description: Flow action for pre-8.4 or non-Cosmos apps using a Rule-HTML-Section. Code generation and button defaults are auto-filled.

nodes.label: flowaction-assignment-preview
nodes.description: How to display external data on a Constellation assignment view with live field-change refresh — architecture, timing, and wiring.

nodes.label: APPEND_AND_MAP_TO
nodes.description: Appends rows from a source page list into a target page list, mapping key fields from each source entry. Requires a DataSource parameter declared in pyPagesAndClasses.

nodes.label: APPLY_MODEL with Parameters
nodes.description: Calls another Data Transform with explicit parameter values.

nodes.label: JSON Format Data Transform — Automap
nodes.description: JSON format Data Transform with automap enabled. All JSON fields are mapped automatically to matching clipboard properties.

nodes.label: JSON Format Data Transform — Nested-Object Descent
nodes.description: Rule-level JSON Data Transform mapping a multi-level response (response → array → object → scalars). Chained UPDATE_PAGE steps with pyUpdateContextOptions "JSON" traverse each nesting level. Demonstrates the working pattern when dotted paths in pyPropertiesValue would be silently skipped.

nodes.label: JSON Format Data Transform — Manual Mapping
nodes.description: JSON format Data Transform with explicit field-level mappings. Uses UPDATE_PAGE with nested SET steps to map JSON fields to clipboard properties.

nodes.label: Primitive Array Extraction — Multiple Fields
nodes.description: Clipboard-format Data Transform extracting multiple primitive JSON array values with guarded pxReplaceAllViaRegex. Repeats the contains() + regex pattern per field — latitude, longitude, and location name from a geocode response.

nodes.label: Primitive Array Extraction — Single Field
nodes.description: Clipboard-format Data Transform extracting one primitive JSON array value with guarded pxReplaceAllViaRegex. Shows the minimal contains() guard + regex pattern for a geocode latitude response.

nodes.label: JSON Response Bridge DT (Clipboard Wrapper)
nodes.description: Clipboard-format wrapper Data Transform that bridges a data page response to a JSON-format DT via APPLY_MODEL. Wire this as pyResDataTransform on the data page — JSON DTs cannot be invoked directly by the data page framework because it does not populate their jsonData parameter.

nodes.label: WHEN / OTHERWISE_WHEN / OTHERWISE
nodes.description: Multi-branch conditional logic (if / else-if / else). OTHERWISE_WHEN adds additional condition branches between the opening WHEN and the final OTHERWISE.

nodes.label: Data Transform pyProperties APPEND New Page
nodes.description: Append single new page with SET children.

nodes.label: Data Transform pyProperties APPEND_TO — Current Source Page
nodes.description: Append the currently iterated page to a target list. Used inside FOR_EACH_PAGE_IN loops.

nodes.label: Data Transform pyProperties APPEND_TO — Existing Page / Page List
nodes.description: Append a copy of an existing page, or all pages from another list.

nodes.label: Data Transform pyProperties APPEND_TO — New Page
nodes.description: Append a single new empty page to a page list. Unlike APPEND_AND_MAP_TO, child steps are optional.

nodes.label: Data Transform pyProperties COMMENT
nodes.description: Developer documentation step (no-op at runtime).

nodes.label: Data Transform pyProperties EXIT_MODEL
nodes.description: Terminates the data transform immediately. All subsequent steps are skipped. Typically used as a child of a WHEN block.

nodes.label: Data Transform pyProperties FOR_EACH_PAGE_IN / EXIT_FOR_EACH
nodes.description: Iterate over each page in a list. Child steps run in the context of each iterated page. EXIT_FOR_EACH breaks out of the loop early.

nodes.label: Data Transform pyProperties JSON Mapping — UPDATE_PAGE with nested SET
nodes.description: Maps a JSON object field to a clipboard page, with child SET steps mapping individual properties. This is an entry in pyMappingModel.pyProperties (Embed-MappingParams).

nodes.label: Data Transform pyProperties JSON nested-object descent — UPDATE_PAGE chain with pyUpdateContextOptions
nodes.description: Step-level JSON nested-descent fix pattern. Chain UPDATE_PAGE steps with pyUpdateContextOptions "JSON" to traverse a multi-level response without moving the clipboard target. Use this instead of dotted paths in pyPropertiesValue (silently skipped at runtime).

nodes.label: Data Transform pyProperties OTHERWISE_WHEN
nodes.description: Else-if branch in a WHEN/OTHERWISE group. Evaluated only when all preceding WHEN and OTHERWISE_WHEN conditions were false.

nodes.label: Data Transform pyProperties REMOVE
nodes.description: Remove a named page from the clipboard.

nodes.label: Data Transform pyProperties SORT
nodes.description: Sort a page list by one or more properties. Sort criteria are defined in pySortActionPropList, not in child steps.

nodes.label: Data Transform pyProperties UPDATE_PAGE with BLANK
nodes.description: Update a page with BLANK initialization. BLANK clears the page's existing data before applying child SET steps, ensuring a known initial state. Use '' (empty string) instead of BLANK to update the page in place without clearing it.

nodes.label: Data Transform pyProperties UPDATE_PAGE with WITH_VALUES_FROM
nodes.description: Copy all properties from a source page to the target page. The source page name goes in pyPropertiesValue (no dot prefix); child pyProperties must be empty.

nodes.label: Data Transform pyProperties WHEN
nodes.description: Conditional block. Child steps run only when the condition expression evaluates true. Use empty pyProperties to test the condition without any actions.

nodes.label: Data Transform pyProperties WHEN with when-rule reference
nodes.description: WHEN step that references a when-rule by name instead of an inline condition expression.

nodes.label: Stub Data Transform
nodes.description: Absolute minimum Clipboard Data Transform create payload — only the three required fields. pyProperties is auto-filled to an empty array by the engine if not passed.

nodes.label: model-json-data-transforms
nodes.description: JSON-format Data Transform behavior — mapping rules, context descent, arrays, primitive array workaround, regex safety, and troubleshooting
